metadata.name: EZ_NATIVE_TRANSFERS
metadata.comment: This convenience table contains all transfers for the chain's native asset, sourced from internal traces (`core.fact_traces`), and includes decimal adjusted and USD values where available. The origin addresses correspond to the to and from addresses in the `core.fact_transactions` table. Note, this table does not contain ERC-721 and ERC-1155 token transfers, instead please use `nft.ez_nft_transfers`.

BLOCK_NUMBER.name: BLOCK_NUMBER
BLOCK_NUMBER.comment: Also known as block height. The block number, which indicates the length of the blockchain, increases after the addition of each new block.

BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.name: BLOCK_TIMESTAMP
BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.comment: The date and time at which the block was produced.

TX_HASH.name: TX_HASH
TX_HASH.comment: Transaction hash is a unique 66-character identifier that is generated when a transaction is executed.

TX_POSITION.name: TX_POSITION
TX_POSITION.comment: The position of the transaction within the block.

TRACE_INDEX.name: TRACE_INDEX
TRACE_INDEX.comment: The index of the trace within the transaction.

TRACE_ADDRESS.name: TRACE_ADDRESS
TRACE_ADDRESS.comment: The trace address for this trace.

TYPE.name: TYPE
TYPE.comment: The type of internal transaction. Common trace types are `CALL`, `DELEGATECALL`, and `STATICCALL`.

FROM_ADDRESS.name: FROM_ADDRESS
FROM_ADDRESS.comment: The sending address of this transfer.

TO_ADDRESS.name: TO_ADDRESS
TO_ADDRESS.comment: The receiving address of this transfer. This can be a contract address.

AMOUNT.name: AMOUNT
AMOUNT.comment: The decimal transformed amount for this token. Tokens without a decimal adjustment will be nulled out here.

AMOUNT_PRECISE_RAW.name: AMOUNT_PRECISE_RAW
AMOUNT_PRECISE_RAW.comment: The amount of tokens transferred returned as a string to preserve precision. This value is not decimal adjusted.

AMOUNT_PRECISE.name: AMOUNT_PRECISE
AMOUNT_PRECISE.comment: The decimal transformed amount for this token returned as a string to preserve precision. Tokens without a decimal adjustment will be nulled out here.

AMOUNT_USD.name: AMOUNT_USD
AMOUNT_USD.comment: Native asset value transferred, in USD.

ORIGIN_FROM_ADDRESS.name: ORIGIN_FROM_ADDRESS
ORIGIN_FROM_ADDRESS.comment: The from address at the transaction level.

ORIGIN_TO_ADDRESS.name: ORIGIN_TO_ADDRESS
ORIGIN_TO_ADDRESS.comment: The to address at the transaction level.

ORIGIN_FUNCTION_SIGNATURE.name: ORIGIN_FUNCTION_SIGNATURE
ORIGIN_FUNCTION_SIGNATURE.comment: The function signature of the call that triggered this transaction.

EZ_NATIVE_TRANSFERS_ID.name: EZ_NATIVE_TRANSFERS_ID
EZ_NATIVE_TRANSFERS_ID.comment: The unique identifier for each row in the table.

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The UTC timestamp at which the row was inserted into the table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The UTC timestamp at which the row was last modified.

metadata.name: EZ_DECODED_EVENT_LOGS
metadata.comment:
For information on how to submit a contract for decoding, as well as how ABIs are sourced, please visit [here](https://science.flipsidecrypto.xyz/abi-requestor/).
      This model contains decoded event logs for contracts that we have an ABI for. Please note, this table does not include all event logs, only those that we have an ABI for.
      The `decoded_log` column is the easiest place to query decoded data. It is a JSON object, where the keys are the names of the event parameters, and the values are the values of the event parameters.
      You can select from this column using the following sample format, `decoded_log:from::string` or more generally, `decoded_log:<event_param>::datatype`. See below for a full sample query.
      The `full_decoded_logs` column contains the same information, as well as additional fields such as the datatype of the decoded data. You may need to laterally flatten this column to query the data.
            
      Sample query for USDC Transfer events:
      
      ```sql
      select 
      tx_hash,
      block_number,
      contract_address,
      decoded_log:from::string as from_address,
      decoded_log:to::string as to_address,
      decoded_log:value::integer as value
      from ethereum.core.fact_decoded_event_logs
      where contract_address = lower('0xA0b86991c6218b36c1d19D4a2e9Eb0cE3606eB48')
      and block_number between 16400000 and 16405000
      and event_name = 'Transfer'
      limit 50```

BLOCK_NUMBER.name: BLOCK_NUMBER
BLOCK_NUMBER.comment: Also known as block height. The block number, which indicates the length of the blockchain, increases after the addition of each new block.

BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.name: BLOCK_TIMESTAMP
BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.comment: The date and time at which the block was produced.

TX_HASH.name: TX_HASH
TX_HASH.comment: Transaction hash is a unique 66-character identifier that is generated when a transaction is executed.

TX_POSITION.name: TX_POSITION
TX_POSITION.comment: The position of the transaction within the block.

CONTRACT_ADDRESS.name: CONTRACT_ADDRESS
CONTRACT_ADDRESS.comment: The address of the contract that the event log was emitted from.

TOPIC_0.name: TOPIC_0
TOPIC_0.comment: The first topic of the event, which is a unique identifier for the event.

TOPIC_1.name: TOPIC_1

metadata.name: DIM_LABELS
metadata.comment: The labels table is a store of one-to-one address identifiers, or an address name. Labels are broken out into a "type" (such as cex, dex, dapp, games, etc.) and a "subtype" (ex: contract_deployer, hot_wallet, token_contract, etc.) in order to help classify each address name into similar groups. Our labels are sourced from many different places, but can primarily be grouped into two categories: automatic and manual. Automatic labels are continuously labeled based on certain criteria, such as a known contract deploying another contract, behavior-based algorithms for finding deposit wallets, and consistent data pulls of custom protocol APIs. Manual labels are done periodically to find addresses that cannot be found programmatically such as finding new protocol addresses, centralized exchange hot wallets, or trending addresses. Labels can also be added by our community by using our add-a-label tool (https://science.flipsidecrypto.xyz/add-a-label/) or on-chain with near (https://near.social/lord1.near/widget/Form) and are reviewed by our labels team. A label can be removed by our labels team if it is found to be incorrect or no longer relevant; this generally will only happen for mislabeled deposit wallets.

CREATOR.name: CREATOR
CREATOR.comment: The name of the creator of the label.

ADDRESS.name: ADDRESS
ADDRESS.comment: Address that the label is for. This is the field that should be used to join other tables with labels.

ADDRESS_NAME.name: ADDRESS_NAME
ADDRESS_NAME.comment: The most granular label for this address.

LABEL_TYPE.name: LABEL_TYPE
LABEL_TYPE.comment: A high-level category describing the address's main function or ownership.

LABEL_SUBTYPE.name: LABEL_SUBTYPE
LABEL_SUBTYPE.comment: A sub-category nested within label type providing further detail.

DIM_LABELS_ID.name: DIM_LABELS_ID
DIM_LABELS_ID.comment: The unique identifier for each row in the table.

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The UTC timestamp at which the row was inserted into the table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The UTC timestamp at which the row was last modified.

metadata.name: FACT_TRACES
metadata.comment: This table contains flattened trace data for internal contract calls. User-defined functions (UDFs) are available to decode hex encoded fields, including `utils.udf_hex_to_int()`.

BLOCK_NUMBER.name: BLOCK_NUMBER
BLOCK_NUMBER.comment: Also known as block height. The block number, which indicates the length of the blockchain, increases after the addition of each new block.

BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.name: BLOCK_TIMESTAMP
BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.comment: The date and time at which the block was produced.

TX_HASH.name: TX_HASH
TX_HASH.comment: Transaction hash is a unique 66-character identifier that is generated when a transaction is executed.

TX_POSITION.name: TX_POSITION
TX_POSITION.comment: The position of the transaction within the block.

TRACE_INDEX.name: TRACE_INDEX
TRACE_INDEX.comment: The index of the trace within the transaction.

FROM_ADDRESS.name: FROM_ADDRESS
FROM_ADDRESS.comment: The sending address of this transaction.

TO_ADDRESS.name: TO_ADDRESS
TO_ADDRESS.comment: The receiving address of this transaction. This can be a contract address.

INPUT.name: INPUT
INPUT.comment: The input data for this trace.

OUTPUT.name: OUTPUT
OUTPUT.comment: The output data for this trace.

TYPE.name: TYPE
TYPE.comment: The type of internal transaction. Common trace types are `CALL`, `DELEGATECALL`, and `STATICCALL`.

TRACE_ADDRESS.name: TRACE_ADDRESS
TRACE_ADDRESS.comment: The trace address for this trace.

SUB_TRACES.name: SUB_TRACES
SUB_TRACES.comment: The amount of nested sub traces for this trace.

VALUE.name: VALUE
VALUE.comment: The value transacted in the native asset.

VALUE_PRECISE_RAW.name: VALUE_PRECISE_RAW
VALUE_PRECISE_RAW.comment: The precise, unadjusted amount of the transaction. This is returned as a string to avoid precision loss.

VALUE_PRECISE.name: VALUE_PRECISE
VALUE_PRECISE.comment: The precise, adjusted amount of the transaction. This is returned as a string to avoid precision loss.

VALUE_HEX.name: VALUE_HEX
VALUE_HEX.comment: The value of the transaction in hexadecimal format.

GAS.name: GAS
GAS.comment: The gas supplied for this trace.

GAS_USED.name: GAS_USED
GAS_USED.comment: The gas used for this trace.

ORIGIN_FROM_ADDRESS.name: ORIGIN_FROM_ADDRESS
ORIGIN_FROM_ADDRESS.comment: The sending address of this trace. This is not necessarily the from address of the transaction.

ORIGIN_TO_ADDRESS.name: ORIGIN_TO_ADDRESS
ORIGIN_TO_ADDRESS.comment: The receiving address of this trace. This is not necessarily the to address of the transaction.

ORIGIN_FUNCTION_SIGNATURE.name: ORIGIN_FUNCTION_SIGNATURE
ORIGIN_FUNCTION_SIGNATURE.comment: The function signature of the contract call that triggered this transaction.

TRACE_SUCCEEDED.name: TRACE_SUCCEEDED
TRACE_SUCCEEDED.comment: The boolean value representing if the trace succeeded.

ERROR_REASON.name: ERROR_REASON
ERROR_REASON.comment: The reason for the trace failure, if any.

REVERT_REASON.name: REVERT_REASON
REVERT_REASON.comment: The reason for the revert, if available.

TX_SUCCEEDED.name: TX_SUCCEEDED
TX_SUCCEEDED.comment: Whether the transaction was successful, returned as a boolean.

FACT_TRACES_ID.name: FACT_TRACES_ID
FACT_TRACES_ID.comment: The unique identifier for each row in the table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The UTC timestamp at which the row was last modified.

metadata.name: EZ_MARKET_STATS
metadata.comment: Orderbook level market stats based on a combination of on-chain data and data from Vertex's ticker V2 API which includes 24-hour pricing and volume information on each market pair available on Vertex.

HOUR.name: HOUR
HOUR.comment: The hour in which the stats table data was pull and inserted into the table.

TICKER_ID.name: TICKER_ID
TICKER_ID.comment: Identifier of a ticker with delimiter to separate base/target.

PRODUCT_ID.name: PRODUCT_ID
PRODUCT_ID.comment: The unique id of each product. Evens are perp products and odds are spot products.

SYMBOL.name: SYMBOL
SYMBOL.comment: The specific Vertex product symbol, if it is a futures product it will have a -PERP suffix.

DISTINCT_SEQUENCER_BATCHES.name: DISTINCT_SEQUENCER_BATCHES
DISTINCT_SEQUENCER_BATCHES.comment: The amount of sequencer transactions that included this product in the last hour.

DISTINCT_TRADER_COUNT.name: DISTINCT_TRADER_COUNT
DISTINCT_TRADER_COUNT.comment: The distinct traders in the last hour, based on a distinct count of wallet addresses.

DISTINCT_SUBACCOUNT_COUNT.name: DISTINCT_SUBACCOUNT_COUNT
DISTINCT_SUBACCOUNT_COUNT.comment: The distinct traders in the last hour, based on a distinct count of subaccount.

TRADE_COUNT.name: TRADE_COUNT
TRADE_COUNT.comment: The total number of trades on Vertex in the last hour.

AMOUNT_USD.name: AMOUNT_USD
AMOUNT_USD.comment: The size of the trade in USD. Base Delta multiplied by the price amount.

FEE_AMOUNT.name: FEE_AMOUNT
FEE_AMOUNT.comment: The fees on the trade, decimal adjusted. All amounts and prices are adjusted 18 decimals points regardless of underlying asset contract.

BASE_DELTA_AMOUNT.name: BASE_DELTA_AMOUNT
BASE_DELTA_AMOUNT.comment: Represents the net change in the total quantity of orders at a particular price level, decimal adjusted. All amounts and prices are adjusted 18 decimals points regardless of underlying asset contract. The sum of these across the same digest is equal to the amount. This is the first currency listed in the pair and acts as the reference point for the exchange rate, in this case the crypto asset trading against USDC.

QUOTE_DELTA_AMOUNT.name: QUOTE_DELTA_AMOUNT
QUOTE_DELTA_AMOUNT.comment: The net change in the best bid and best ask prices in the order book, decimal adjusted. All amounts and prices are adjusted 18 decimals points regardless of underlying asset contract. A positive value is an increase in spread and a negative value is a decrease in spread. Quote is currency used to express the value of the base currency. It's often the more well-known or stable currency in the pair. In this case, USDC.

BASE_VOLUME_24H.name: BASE_VOLUME_24H
BASE_VOLUME_24H.comment: The 24 hour trading volume for the pair (unit in base).

QUOTE_VOLUME_24H.name: QUOTE_VOLUME_24H
QUOTE_VOLUME_24H.comment: The 24 hour trading volume for the pair (unit in quote).

FUNDING_RATE.name: FUNDING_RATE
FUNDING_RATE.comment:
Current 24hr funding rate. Can compute hourly funding rate dividing by 24.

A funding rate is a mechanism used to ensure that the price of a perp contract tracks the underlying asset's price as closely as possible.

Positive funding rates reflect the perpetual trading at a premium to the underlying asset’s price.

INDEX_PRICE.name: INDEX_PRICE
INDEX_PRICE.comment: Last calculated index price for underlying of contract.

LAST_PRICE.name: LAST_PRICE
LAST_PRICE.comment: Last transacted price of base currency based on given quote currency.

MARK_PRICE.name: MARK_PRICE
MARK_PRICE.comment: The calculated fair value of the contract, independent of the last traded price on the specific exchange.

NEXT_FUNDING_RATE_TIMESTAMP.name: NEXT_FUNDING_RATE_TIMESTAMP
NEXT_FUNDING_RATE_TIMESTAMP.comment: Timestamp of the next funding rate change, specific to hour the data was pulled from the API.

OPEN_INTEREST.name: OPEN_INTEREST
OPEN_INTEREST.comment: The open interest of the contract for the hour that the data was pulled. Open interest (OI) refers to the total number of outstanding derivative contracts (e.g., futures or options) that are currently held by market participants and have not yet been settled

OPEN_INTEREST_USD.name: OPEN_INTEREST_USD
OPEN_INTEREST_USD.comment: The open interest of the contract for the hour that the data was pulled, denominated in USD. Open interest (OI) refers to the total number of outstanding derivative contracts (e.g., futures or options) that are currently held by market participants and have not yet been settled

PRODUCT_TYPE.name: PRODUCT_TYPE
PRODUCT_TYPE.comment: The type of product, either spot or perpetual futures.

EZ_MARKET_STATS_ID.name: EZ_MARKET_STATS_ID
EZ_MARKET_STATS_ID.comment: The unique identifier for each row in the table.

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The UTC timestamp at which the row was inserted into the table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The UTC timestamp at which the row was last modified.

BLOCK_NUMBER.comment: Also known as block height. The block number, which indicates the length of the blockchain, increases after the addition of each new block.

BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.name: BLOCK_TIMESTAMP

TX_HASH.name: TX_HASH

metadata.name: EZ_ACCOUNT_STATS
metadata.comment: Subaccount level table showing aggregated total activity across the Vertex exchange.

SUBACCOUNT.name: SUBACCOUNT
SUBACCOUNT.comment: Independent Vertex account of trader with its own margin, balance, positions, and trades. Any wallet can open an arbitrary number of these. Risk is not carried over from subaccount to subaccount.

TRADER.name: TRADER
TRADER.comment: The wallet address of the trader, there can be multiple subaccounts associated with a trader.

FIRST_TRADE_TIMESTAMP.name: FIRST_TRADE_TIMESTAMP
FIRST_TRADE_TIMESTAMP.comment: The block timestamp of this subaccounts first trade.

LAST_TRADE_TIMESTAMP.name: LAST_TRADE_TIMESTAMP
LAST_TRADE_TIMESTAMP.comment: The block timestamp of this subaccounts most recent trade.

ACCOUNT_AGE.name: ACCOUNT_AGE
ACCOUNT_AGE.comment: The age of the account in days.

TRADE_COUNT.name: TRADE_COUNT
TRADE_COUNT.comment: The total amount of trades executed by the account

TRADE_COUNT_RANK.name: TRADE_COUNT_RANK
TRADE_COUNT_RANK.comment: The rank against all accounts based on trade count volume.

TRADE_COUNT_24H.name: TRADE_COUNT_24H
TRADE_COUNT_24H.comment: The total amount of trades made in the last 24 hours.

TRADE_COUNT_RANK_24H.name: TRADE_COUNT_RANK_24H
TRADE_COUNT_RANK_24H.comment: The rank against all accounts based on trade count volume in the last 24 hours.

PERP_TRADE_COUNT.name: PERP_TRADE_COUNT
PERP_TRADE_COUNT.comment: The total amount of perpetual trades executed by the account

SPOT_TRADE_COUNT.name: SPOT_TRADE_COUNT
SPOT_TRADE_COUNT.comment: The total amount of spot trades executed by the account

LONG_COUNT.name: LONG_COUNT
LONG_COUNT.comment: The total amount of buys/longs on the account.

SHORT_COUNT.name: SHORT_COUNT
SHORT_COUNT.comment: The total amount of sell/shorts on the account.

TOTAL_USD_VOLUME.name: TOTAL_USD_VOLUME
TOTAL_USD_VOLUME.comment: The total USD denominated volume of the account.

TOTAL_USD_VOLUME_RANK.name: TOTAL_USD_VOLUME_RANK
TOTAL_USD_VOLUME_RANK.comment: The rank against all accounts based on total usd volume on the account.

TOTAL_USD_VOLUME_24H.name: TOTAL_USD_VOLUME_24H
TOTAL_USD_VOLUME_24H.comment: The total USD denominated volume of the account in the last 24 hours.

TOTAL_USD_VOLUME_RANK_24H.name: TOTAL_USD_VOLUME_RANK_24H
TOTAL_USD_VOLUME_RANK_24H.comment: The rank against all accounts based on the total USD denominated volume of the account in the last 24 hours.

AVG_USD_TRADE_SIZE.name: AVG_USD_TRADE_SIZE
AVG_USD_TRADE_SIZE.comment: The average trade size in USD.

TOTAL_FEE_AMOUNT.name: TOTAL_FEE_AMOUNT
TOTAL_FEE_AMOUNT.comment: The total amount of trading fees paid by the account.

TOTAL_BASE_DELTA_AMOUNT.name: TOTAL_BASE_DELTA_AMOUNT
TOTAL_BASE_DELTA_AMOUNT.comment: The total base delta amount of the account.

TOTAL_QUOTE_DELTA_AMOUNT.name: TOTAL_QUOTE_DELTA_AMOUNT
TOTAL_QUOTE_DELTA_AMOUNT.comment: The total quote delta amount of the account.

TOTAL_LIQUIDATION_AMOUNT.name: TOTAL_LIQUIDATION_AMOUNT
TOTAL_LIQUIDATION_AMOUNT.comment: The total liquidation amount of the account.

TOTAL_LIQUIDATION_AMOUNT_QUOTE.name: TOTAL_LIQUIDATION_AMOUNT_QUOTE
TOTAL_LIQUIDATION_AMOUNT_QUOTE.comment: To liquidate a position, there must be a payment (transfer) between the liquidator and the position holder. This done in the quote currency, USDC. Payments are signed as positive, meaning you received the USDC, or negative, meaning you paid. For perpetual liquidations, users should expect to see a (+) USDC payment. They will see a (-) USDC payment for borrowers since they need to pay the user for buying their borrow.

TOTAL_LIQUIDATION_COUNT.name: TOTAL_LIQUIDATION_COUNT
TOTAL_LIQUIDATION_COUNT.comment: The total count of liquidation accounts on the account.

EZ_ACCOUNT_STATS_ID.name: EZ_ACCOUNT_STATS_ID
EZ_ACCOUNT_STATS_ID.comment: The unique identifier for each row in the table.

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The UTC timestamp at which the row was inserted into the table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The UTC timestamp at which the row was last modified.

metadata.name: EZ_LIQUIDATIONS
metadata.comment: All Vertex liquidations. Once an account’s maintenance margin reaches [$]0, the account is eligible for liquidation. Liquidation events happen one by one, with the riskiest positions being liquidated first. Liquidations are based on the oracle price.

BLOCK_NUMBER.name: BLOCK_NUMBER
BLOCK_NUMBER.comment: Also known as block height. The block number, which indicates the length of the blockchain, increases after the addition of each new block.

BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.name: BLOCK_TIMESTAMP
BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.comment: The date and time at which the block was produced.

TX_HASH.name: TX_HASH
TX_HASH.comment: Transaction hash is a unique 66-character identifier that is generated when a transaction is executed. This field will not be unique in this table, as a given transaction can include multiple events.

CONTRACT_ADDRESS.name: CONTRACT_ADDRESS
CONTRACT_ADDRESS.comment: The address interacted with for a given event.

EVENT_NAME.name: EVENT_NAME
EVENT_NAME.comment: The name of the event, as defined in the contract ABI.

ORIGIN_FUNCTION_SIGNATURE.name: ORIGIN_FUNCTION_SIGNATURE
ORIGIN_FUNCTION_SIGNATURE.comment: The function signature of the contract call that triggered this transaction.

ORIGIN_FROM_ADDRESS.name: ORIGIN_FROM_ADDRESS
ORIGIN_FROM_ADDRESS.comment: The from address at the transaction level.

ORIGIN_TO_ADDRESS.name: ORIGIN_TO_ADDRESS
ORIGIN_TO_ADDRESS.comment: The to address at the transaction level.

DIGEST.name: DIGEST
DIGEST.comment: The identifier for a specific trade, this can be split across two or more base deltas in order to fill the entire amount of the trade.

TRADER.name: TRADER
TRADER.comment: The wallet address of the trader, there can be multiple subaccounts associated with a trader.

SUBACCOUNT.name: SUBACCOUNT
SUBACCOUNT.comment: Independent Vertex account of trader with its own margin, balance, positions, and trades. Any wallet can open an arbitrary number of these. Risk is not carried over from subaccount to subaccount.

PRODUCT_ID.name: PRODUCT_ID
PRODUCT_ID.comment:
The product to liquidate as well as the liquidation mode:
Perp Liquidation: Any valid perp product_id with is_encode_spread set to false. 
Spot Liquidation: Any valid spot product_id with is_encode_spread set to false. 
Spread Liquidation: If there are perp and spot positions in different directions, liquidate both at the same time. is_encode_spread must be set to true.

If it is a spread liquidation this column will show the perp product_id, for both ids refer to the spread_product_ids array.

Only available in V2 Vertex liquidations, which went live March 8th 2024.

HEALTH_GROUP.name: HEALTH_GROUP
HEALTH_GROUP.comment: The spot / perp product pair of health group i where health_groups[i][0] is the spot product_id and health_groups[i][1] is the perp product_id. Additionally, it is possible for a health group to only have either a spot or perp product, in which case, the product that doesn’t exist is set to 0.

HEALTH_GROUP_SYMBOL.name: HEALTH_GROUP_SYMBOL
HEALTH_GROUP_SYMBOL.comment: The token symbol represented by the specific health group. For example WBTC and BTC-PERP is BTC.

AMOUNT_UNADJ.name: AMOUNT_UNADJ
AMOUNT_UNADJ.comment: The total size of the trade in units of the asset being traded.

AMOUNT.name: AMOUNT
AMOUNT.comment: The total size of the trade in units of the asset being traded across one digest, decimal adjusted. All amounts and prices are adjusted 18 decimals points regardless of underlying asset contract.

AMOUNT_QUOTE_UNADJ.name: AMOUNT_QUOTE_UNADJ
AMOUNT_QUOTE_UNADJ.comment: To liquidate a position, there must be a payment (transfer) between the liquidator and the position holder. This done in the quote currency, USDC. Payments are signed as positive, meaning you received the USDC, or negative, meaning you paid. For perpetual liquidations, users should expect to see a (+) USDC payment. They will see a (-) USDC payment for borrowers since they need to pay the user for buying their borrow.

AMOUNT_QUOTE.name: AMOUNT_QUOTE
AMOUNT_QUOTE.comment: To liquidate a position, there must be a payment (transfer) between the liquidator and the position holder. This done in the quote currency, USDC. Payments are signed as positive, meaning you received the USDC, or negative, meaning you paid. For perpetual liquidations, users should expect to see a (+) USDC payment. They will see a (-) USDC payment for borrowers since they need to pay the user for buying their borrow. All amounts and prices are adjusted 18 decimals points regardless of underlying asset contract.

SPREAD_PRODUCT_IDS.name: SPREAD_PRODUCT_IDS
SPREAD_PRODUCT_IDS.comment: Array of product_ids that have been decoded from binary. Only available when is_encode_spread is true and the liquidation occurs on V2 Vertex, which went live March 8th 2024.

EZ_LIQUIDATIONS_ID.name: EZ_LIQUIDATIONS_ID
EZ_LIQUIDATIONS_ID.comment: The unique identifier for each row in the table.

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The UTC timestamp at which the row was inserted into the table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The UTC timestamp at which the row was last modified.

metadata.name: EZ_PERP_TRADES
metadata.comment: Vertex perpetuals are derivative contracts on an underlying spot asset. On Vertex, all perpetual contracts trade against USDC.

BLOCK_NUMBER.name: BLOCK_NUMBER
BLOCK_NUMBER.comment: Also known as block height. The block number, which indicates the length of the blockchain, increases after the addition of each new block.

BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.name: BLOCK_TIMESTAMP
BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.comment: The date and time at which the block was produced.

TX_HASH.name: TX_HASH
TX_HASH.comment: Transaction hash is a unique 66-character identifier that is generated when a transaction is executed. This field will not be unique in this table, as a given transaction can include multiple events.

CONTRACT_ADDRESS.name: CONTRACT_ADDRESS
CONTRACT_ADDRESS.comment: The address interacted with for a given event.

EVENT_NAME.name: EVENT_NAME
EVENT_NAME.comment: The name of the event, as defined in the contract ABI.

ORIGIN_FUNCTION_SIGNATURE.name: ORIGIN_FUNCTION_SIGNATURE
ORIGIN_FUNCTION_SIGNATURE.comment: The function signature of the contract call that triggered this transaction.

ORIGIN_FROM_ADDRESS.name: ORIGIN_FROM_ADDRESS
ORIGIN_FROM_ADDRESS.comment: The from address at the transaction level.

ORIGIN_TO_ADDRESS.name: ORIGIN_TO_ADDRESS
ORIGIN_TO_ADDRESS.comment: The to address at the transaction level.

SYMBOL.name: SYMBOL
SYMBOL.comment: The specific Vertex product symbol, if it is a futures product it will have a -PERP suffix.

DIGEST.name: DIGEST
DIGEST.comment: The identifier for a specific trade, this can be split across two or more base deltas in order to fill the entire amount of the trade.

TRADER.name: TRADER
TRADER.comment: The wallet address of the trader, there can be multiple subaccounts associated with a trader.

SUBACCOUNT.name: SUBACCOUNT
SUBACCOUNT.comment: Independent Vertex account of trader with its own margin, balance, positions, and trades. Any wallet can open an arbitrary number of these. Risk is not carried over from subaccount to subaccount.

TRADE_TYPE.name: TRADE_TYPE
TRADE_TYPE.comment: They type of trade taken, long/short for perps or buy/sell for spot.

ORDER_TYPE.name: ORDER_TYPE
ORDER_TYPE.comment:
Decode from raw expiration number to binary then converted back to int from the most significant two bits: 
0 ⇒ Default order, where it will attempt to take from the book and then become a resting limit order if there is quantity remaining
1 ⇒ Immediate-or-cancel order, which is the same as a default order except it doesn’t become a resting limit order
2 ⇒ Fill-or-kill order, which is the same as an IOC order except either the entire order has to be filled or none of it.
3 ⇒ Post-only order, where the order is not allowed to take from the book. An error is returned if the order would cross the bid ask spread.

MARKET_REDUCE_FLAG.name: MARKET_REDUCE_FLAG
MARKET_REDUCE_FLAG.comment: A reduce-only is an order that will either close or reduce your position. The reduce-only flag can only be set on IOC or FOK order types. Send a reduce-only order by setting the 3rd most significant bit on the expiration field.

EXPIRATION.name: EXPIRATION
EXPIRATION.comment: Time after which the order should automatically be cancelled, as a timestamp in seconds after the unix epoch, converted to datetime.

NONCE.name: NONCE
NONCE.comment: Number used to differentiate between the same order multiple times, and a user trying to place an order with the same parameters twice. Represented as a string.

IS_TAKER.name: IS_TAKER
IS_TAKER.comment: Boolean representing if the trader was the taker or maker.

PRICE_AMOUNT_UNADJ.name: PRICE_AMOUNT_UNADJ
PRICE_AMOUNT_UNADJ.comment: The price amount that the trade was executed at.

PRICE_AMOUNT.name: PRICE_AMOUNT
PRICE_AMOUNT.comment: The price amount that the trade was executed at, decimal adjusted. All amounts and prices are adjusted 18 decimals points regardless of underlying asset contract.

AMOUNT_UNADJ.name: AMOUNT_UNADJ
AMOUNT_UNADJ.comment: The total size of the trade in units of the asset being traded.

AMOUNT.name: AMOUNT
AMOUNT.comment: The total size of the trade in units of the asset being traded across one digest, decimal adjusted. All amounts and prices are adjusted 18 decimals points regardless of underlying asset contract.

AMOUNT_USD.name: AMOUNT_USD
AMOUNT_USD.comment: The size of the trade in USD. Base Delta multiplied by the price amount.

FEE_AMOUNT.name: FEE_AMOUNT
FEE_AMOUNT.comment: The fees on the trade, decimal adjusted. All amounts and prices are adjusted 18 decimals points regardless of underlying asset contract.

BASE_DELTA_AMOUNT_UNADJ.name: BASE_DELTA_AMOUNT_UNADJ
BASE_DELTA_AMOUNT_UNADJ.comment: Represents the net change in the total quantity of orders at a particular price level, the sum of these across the same digest is equal to the amount. This is the first currency listed in the pair and acts as the reference point for the exchange rate, in this case the crypto asset trading against USDC.

BASE_DELTA_AMOUNT.name: BASE_DELTA_AMOUNT
BASE_DELTA_AMOUNT.comment: Represents the net change in the total quantity of orders at a particular price level, decimal adjusted. All amounts and prices are adjusted 18 decimals points regardless of underlying asset contract. The sum of these across the same digest is equal to the amount. This is the first currency listed in the pair and acts as the reference point for the exchange rate, in this case the crypto asset trading against USDC.

QUOTE_DELTA_AMOUNT_UNADJ.name: QUOTE_DELTA_AMOUNT_UNADJ
QUOTE_DELTA_AMOUNT_UNADJ.comment: A positive value is an increase in spread and a negative value is a decrease in spread. Quote is currency used to express the value of the base currency. It's often the more well-known or stable currency in the pair. In this case, USDC.

QUOTE_DELTA_AMOUNT.name: QUOTE_DELTA_AMOUNT
QUOTE_DELTA_AMOUNT.comment: The net change in the best bid and best ask prices in the order book, decimal adjusted. All amounts and prices are adjusted 18 decimals points regardless of underlying asset contract. A positive value is an increase in spread and a negative value is a decrease in spread. Quote is currency used to express the value of the base currency. It's often the more well-known or stable currency in the pair. In this case, USDC.

EZ_PERP_TRADES_ID.name: EZ_PERP_TRADES_ID
EZ_PERP_TRADES_ID.comment: The unique identifier for each row in the table.

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The UTC timestamp at which the row was inserted into the table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The UTC timestamp at which the row was last modified.

metadata.name: EZ_MONEY_MARKETS
metadata.comment: Vertex integrates a decentralized money market directly into its DEX, enabling borrowing and lending of crypto assets using overcollateralized lending rules. Interest rates are dynamically adjusted based on supply and demand, incentivizing liquidity provision and balancing borrowing costs. The money market operates on-chain (e.g., on Arbitrum) and is managed through Vertex’s risk engine and clearinghouse, offering users automated borrowing via portfolio margin and passive yield opportunities on idle assets. This table tracks the money market products available on Vertex on an hourly basis.

HOUR.name: HOUR
HOUR.comment: The hour in which the stats table data was pull and inserted into the table.

TICKER_ID.name: TICKER_ID
TICKER_ID.comment: Identifier of a ticker with delimiter to separate base/target.

SYMBOL.name: SYMBOL
SYMBOL.comment: The specific Vertex product symbol, if it is a futures product it will have a -PERP suffix.

PRODUCT_ID.name: PRODUCT_ID
PRODUCT_ID.comment: The unique id of each product. Evens are perp products and odds are spot products.

DEPOSIT_APR.name: DEPOSIT_APR
DEPOSIT_APR.comment: The recorded deposit APR for the money market product in that hour.

BORROW_APR.name: BORROW_APR
BORROW_APR.comment: The recorded borrow APR for the money market product in that hour.

TVL.name: TVL
TVL.comment: The sum total value locked for the money market product in that hour.

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The UTC timestamp at which the row was inserted into the table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The UTC timestamp at which the row was last modified.

metadata.name: EZ_PRICES_HOURLY
metadata.comment: A convenience table for determining token prices by address and blockchain, and native asset prices by symbol and blockchain. This data set is highly curated and contains metadata for one price per hour per unique asset and blockchain.

HOUR.name: HOUR
HOUR.comment: The hour at which the token price was recorded on this EVM blockchain.

TOKEN_ADDRESS.name: TOKEN_ADDRESS
TOKEN_ADDRESS.comment: The specific address representing the asset on this EVM blockchain. This will be NULL if referring to a native asset.

DECIMALS.name: DECIMALS
DECIMALS.comment: The number of decimals for the token contract on this EVM blockchain.

PRICE.name: PRICE
PRICE.comment: The token price for a given hour on this EVM blockchain.

BLOCKCHAIN.name: BLOCKCHAIN
BLOCKCHAIN.comment: The Blockchain, Network, or Platform for this asset.

IS_NATIVE.name: IS_NATIVE
IS_NATIVE.comment: A flag indicating assets native to this EVM blockchain.

IS_IMPUTED.name: IS_IMPUTED
IS_IMPUTED.comment: A flag indicating if the price was imputed, or derived, from the last arriving record. This is generally used for tokens with low-liquidity or inconsistent reporting.

IS_DEPRECATED.name: IS_DEPRECATED
IS_DEPRECATED.comment: A flag indicating if the asset is deprecated or no longer supported by the provider.

EZ_PRICES_HOURLY_ID.name: EZ_PRICES_HOURLY_ID
EZ_PRICES_HOURLY_ID.comment: The unique identifier for each row in the table.

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The UTC timestamp at which the row was inserted into the table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The UTC timestamp at which the row was last modified.

metadata.name: EZ_SPOT_TRADES
metadata.comment: Vertex’s spot markets allow you to buy or sell listed crypto assets paired with USD-denominated stablecoins.

BLOCK_NUMBER.name: BLOCK_NUMBER
BLOCK_NUMBER.comment: Also known as block height. The block number, which indicates the length of the blockchain, increases after the addition of each new block.

BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.name: BLOCK_TIMESTAMP
BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.comment: The date and time at which the block was produced.

TX_HASH.name: TX_HASH
TX_HASH.comment: Transaction hash is a unique 66-character identifier that is generated when a transaction is executed. This field will not be unique in this table, as a given transaction can include multiple events.

CONTRACT_ADDRESS.name: CONTRACT_ADDRESS
CONTRACT_ADDRESS.comment: The address interacted with for a given event.

EVENT_NAME.name: EVENT_NAME
EVENT_NAME.comment: The name of the event, as defined in the contract ABI.

ORIGIN_FUNCTION_SIGNATURE.name: ORIGIN_FUNCTION_SIGNATURE
ORIGIN_FUNCTION_SIGNATURE.comment: The function signature of the contract call that triggered this transaction.

ORIGIN_FROM_ADDRESS.name: ORIGIN_FROM_ADDRESS
ORIGIN_FROM_ADDRESS.comment: The from address at the transaction level.

ORIGIN_TO_ADDRESS.name: ORIGIN_TO_ADDRESS
ORIGIN_TO_ADDRESS.comment: The to address at the transaction level.

SYMBOL.name: SYMBOL
SYMBOL.comment: The specific Vertex product symbol, if it is a futures product it will have a -PERP suffix.

DIGEST.name: DIGEST
DIGEST.comment: The identifier for a specific trade, this can be split across two or more base deltas in order to fill the entire amount of the trade.

TRADER.name: TRADER
TRADER.comment: The wallet address of the trader, there can be multiple subaccounts associated with a trader.

SUBACCOUNT.name: SUBACCOUNT
SUBACCOUNT.comment: Independent Vertex account of trader with its own margin, balance, positions, and trades. Any wallet can open an arbitrary number of these. Risk is not carried over from subaccount to subaccount.

TRADE_TYPE.name: TRADE_TYPE
TRADE_TYPE.comment: They type of trade taken, long/short for perps or buy/sell for spot.

ORDER_TYPE.name: ORDER_TYPE
ORDER_TYPE.comment:
Decode from raw expiration number to binary then converted back to int from the most significant two bits: 
0 ⇒ Default order, where it will attempt to take from the book and then become a resting limit order if there is quantity remaining
1 ⇒ Immediate-or-cancel order, which is the same as a default order except it doesn’t become a resting limit order
2 ⇒ Fill-or-kill order, which is the same as an IOC order except either the entire order has to be filled or none of it.
3 ⇒ Post-only order, where the order is not allowed to take from the book. An error is returned if the order would cross the bid ask spread.

MARKET_REDUCE_FLAG.name: MARKET_REDUCE_FLAG
MARKET_REDUCE_FLAG.comment: A reduce-only is an order that will either close or reduce your position. The reduce-only flag can only be set on IOC or FOK order types. Send a reduce-only order by setting the 3rd most significant bit on the expiration field.

EXPIRATION.name: EXPIRATION
EXPIRATION.comment: Time after which the order should automatically be cancelled, as a timestamp in seconds after the unix epoch, converted to datetime.

NONCE.name: NONCE
NONCE.comment: Number used to differentiate between the same order multiple times, and a user trying to place an order with the same parameters twice. Represented as a string.

IS_TAKER.name: IS_TAKER
IS_TAKER.comment: Boolean representing if the trader was the taker or maker.

PRICE_AMOUNT_UNADJ.name: PRICE_AMOUNT_UNADJ
PRICE_AMOUNT_UNADJ.comment: The price amount that the trade was executed at.

PRICE_AMOUNT.name: PRICE_AMOUNT
PRICE_AMOUNT.comment: The price amount that the trade was executed at, decimal adjusted. All amounts and prices are adjusted 18 decimals points regardless of underlying asset contract.

AMOUNT_UNADJ.name: AMOUNT_UNADJ
AMOUNT_UNADJ.comment: The total size of the trade in units of the asset being traded.

AMOUNT.name: AMOUNT
AMOUNT.comment: The total size of the trade in units of the asset being traded across one digest, decimal adjusted. All amounts and prices are adjusted 18 decimals points regardless of underlying asset contract.

AMOUNT_USD.name: AMOUNT_USD
AMOUNT_USD.comment: The size of the trade in USD. Base Delta multiplied by the price amount.

FEE_AMOUNT.name: FEE_AMOUNT
FEE_AMOUNT.comment: The fees on the trade, decimal adjusted. All amounts and prices are adjusted 18 decimals points regardless of underlying asset contract.

BASE_DELTA_AMOUNT_UNADJ.name: BASE_DELTA_AMOUNT_UNADJ
BASE_DELTA_AMOUNT_UNADJ.comment: Represents the net change in the total quantity of orders at a particular price level, the sum of these across the same digest is equal to the amount. This is the first currency listed in the pair and acts as the reference point for the exchange rate, in this case the crypto asset trading against USDC.

BASE_DELTA_AMOUNT.name: BASE_DELTA_AMOUNT
BASE_DELTA_AMOUNT.comment: Represents the net change in the total quantity of orders at a particular price level, decimal adjusted. All amounts and prices are adjusted 18 decimals points regardless of underlying asset contract. The sum of these across the same digest is equal to the amount. This is the first currency listed in the pair and acts as the reference point for the exchange rate, in this case the crypto asset trading against USDC.

QUOTE_DELTA_AMOUNT_UNADJ.name: QUOTE_DELTA_AMOUNT_UNADJ
QUOTE_DELTA_AMOUNT_UNADJ.comment: A positive value is an increase in spread and a negative value is a decrease in spread. Quote is currency used to express the value of the base currency. It's often the more well-known or stable currency in the pair. In this case, USDC.

QUOTE_DELTA_AMOUNT.name: QUOTE_DELTA_AMOUNT
QUOTE_DELTA_AMOUNT.comment: The net change in the best bid and best ask prices in the order book, decimal adjusted. All amounts and prices are adjusted 18 decimals points regardless of underlying asset contract. A positive value is an increase in spread and a negative value is a decrease in spread. Quote is currency used to express the value of the base currency. It's often the more well-known or stable currency in the pair. In this case, USDC.

EZ_SPOT_TRADES_ID.name: EZ_SPOT_TRADES_ID
EZ_SPOT_TRADES_ID.comment: The unique identifier for each row in the table.

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The UTC timestamp at which the row was inserted into the table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The UTC timestamp at which the row was last modified.

metadata.comment: Liquidity data taken from Vertex's Orderbook API, showing amount of liquidity at each price level.

HOUR.name: HOUR
HOUR.comment: The hour in which the stats table data was pull and inserted into the table.

TICKER_ID.name: TICKER_ID
TICKER_ID.comment: Identifier of a ticker with delimiter to separate base/target.

PRODUCT_ID.name: PRODUCT_ID
PRODUCT_ID.comment: The unique id of each product. Evens are perp products and odds are spot products.

ORDERBOOK_SIDE.name: ORDERBOOK_SIDE
ORDERBOOK_SIDE.comment: Designates the bid or ask side of the orderbook price.

VOLUME.name: VOLUME
VOLUME.comment: The quantity for each bid/ask order at the given price level.

PRICE.name: PRICE
PRICE.comment: The price level for each bid/ask order.

ROUND_PRICE_0_01.name: ROUND_PRICE_0_01
ROUND_PRICE_0_01.comment: The price level for each bid/ask order, rounded to nearest cent.

ROUND_PRICE_0_1.name: ROUND_PRICE_0_1
ROUND_PRICE_0_1.comment: The price level for each bid/ask order, rounded to nearest ten cents.

ROUND_PRICE_1.name: ROUND_PRICE_1
ROUND_PRICE_1.comment: The price level for each bid/ask order, rounded to nearest dollar.

ROUND_PRICE_10.name: ROUND_PRICE_10
ROUND_PRICE_10.comment: The price level for each bid/ask order, rounded to nearest 10 dollars.

ROUND_PRICE_100.name: ROUND_PRICE_100
ROUND_PRICE_100.comment: The price level for each bid/ask order, rounded to nearest 100 dollars.

EZ_MARKET_DEPTH_STATS_ID.name: EZ_MARKET_DEPTH_STATS_ID
EZ_MARKET_DEPTH_STATS_ID.comment: The unique identifier for each row in the table.

metadata.name: EZ_TOKEN_TRANSFERS
metadata.comment: This convenience table contains emitted event logs for ERC-20 Token Transfers (e.g. `Transfer`: topic_0 = `0xddf252ad1be2c89b69c2b068fc378daa952ba7f163c4a11628f55a4df523b3ef`), including decimal adjusted values, USD values, and other helpful token metadata where available for each transfer event. The contract address is the token transferred, and the raw amount field is the amount of tokens transferred. Note, this table does not contain ERC-721 and ERC-1155 token transfers, instead please use `nft.ez_nft_transfers`. Additionally, this table does not contain transfers of the chain's native asset, instead please use `core.ez_native_transfers`.

BLOCK_NUMBER.name: BLOCK_NUMBER
BLOCK_NUMBER.comment: Also known as block height. The block number, which indicates the length of the blockchain, increases after the addition of each new block.

BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.name: BLOCK_TIMESTAMP
BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.comment: The date and time at which the block was produced.

TX_HASH.name: TX_HASH
TX_HASH.comment: Transaction hash is a unique 66-character identifier that is generated when a transaction is executed.

TX_POSITION.name: TX_POSITION
TX_POSITION.comment: The position of the transaction within the block.

FROM_ADDRESS.name: FROM_ADDRESS
FROM_ADDRESS.comment: The sending address of this transfer.

TO_ADDRESS.name: TO_ADDRESS
TO_ADDRESS.comment: The receiving address of this transfer. This can be a contract address.

CONTRACT_ADDRESS.name: CONTRACT_ADDRESS
CONTRACT_ADDRESS.comment: The address of the contract that the event log was emitted from.

TOKEN_STANDARD.name: TOKEN_STANDARD
TOKEN_STANDARD.comment: The type of token transferred in this event, for example `erc721`, `erc1155`, or `erc20`.

NAME.name: NAME
NAME.comment: The name of the deployed contract. Please note this is not necessarily unique.

SYMBOL.name: SYMBOL
SYMBOL.comment: The symbol used to represent this contract. Please note this is not necessarily unique.

DECIMALS.name: DECIMALS
DECIMALS.comment: The decimals for the token contract.

RAW_AMOUNT_PRECISE.name: RAW_AMOUNT_PRECISE
RAW_AMOUNT_PRECISE.comment: The amount of tokens transferred returned as a string to preserve precision. This value is not decimal adjusted.

RAW_AMOUNT.name: RAW_AMOUNT
RAW_AMOUNT.comment: The amount of tokens transferred. This value is not decimal adjusted.

AMOUNT_PRECISE.name: AMOUNT_PRECISE
AMOUNT_PRECISE.comment: The decimal transformed amount for this token returned as a string to preserve precision. Tokens without a decimal adjustment will be nulled out here.

AMOUNT.name: AMOUNT
AMOUNT.comment: The decimal transformed amount for this token. Tokens without a decimal adjustment will be nulled out here.

AMOUNT_USD.name: AMOUNT_USD

metadata.name: DIM_ASSET_METADATA
metadata.comment: A comprehensive dimensional table holding asset metadata and other relevant details pertaining to each id, from multiple providers. This data set includes raw, non-transformed data coming directly from the provider APIs and rows are not intended to be unique. As a result, there may be data quality issues persisting in the APIs that flow through to this dimensional model. If you are interested in using a curated data set instead, please utilize ez_asset_metadata.

metadata.name: FACT_PRICES_OHLC_HOURLY
metadata.comment: A comprehensive fact table holding id and provider specific open, high, low, close hourly prices, from multiple providers. This data set includes raw, non-transformed data coming directly from the provider APIs and rows are not intended to be unique. As a result, there may be data quality issues persisting in the APIs that flow through to this fact-based model. If you are interested in using a curated data set instead, please utilize ez_prices_hourly.

ASSET_ID.name: ASSET_ID
ASSET_ID.comment: The unique identifier representing the asset.

HOUR.name: HOUR
HOUR.comment: The hour at which the token price was recorded on this EVM blockchain.

OPEN.name: OPEN
OPEN.comment: Opening price of the recorded hour in USD.

HIGH.name: HIGH
HIGH.comment: Highest price of the recorded hour in USD.

LOW.name: LOW
LOW.comment: Lowest price of the recorded hour in USD.

CLOSE.name: CLOSE
CLOSE.comment: Closing price of the recorded hour in USD.

FACT_PRICES_OHLC_HOURLY_ID.name: FACT_PRICES_OHLC_HOURLY_ID
FACT_PRICES_OHLC_HOURLY_ID.comment: The unique identifier for each row in the table.

metadata.name: FACT_TRANSACTIONS
metadata.comment:
This table contains transaction level data for this EVM blockchain. Each transaction will have a unique transaction hash, along with transaction fees and a value transferred in the native asset when applicable. Transactions may be native asset transfers or interactions with contract addresses. For more information, please see [The Ethereum Organization - Transactions](https://ethereum.org/en/developers/docs/transactions/)

Below are the specific native tokens that correspond to each EVM chain:

| Status     | Description |
|------------|-------------|
| ETHEREUM   | ETH         |
| BINANCE    | BNB         |
| POLYGON    | POL         |
| AVALANCHE  | AVAX        |
| ARBITRUM   | ETH         |
| OPTIMISM   | ETH         |
| GNOSIS     | xDAI        |
| KAIA       | KLAY        |
| SEI        | SEI         |
| CORE       | CORE        |

BLOCK_NUMBER.name: BLOCK_NUMBER
BLOCK_NUMBER.comment: Also known as block height. The block number, which indicates the length of the blockchain, increases after the addition of each new block.

BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.name: BLOCK_TIMESTAMP
BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.comment: The date and time at which the block was produced.

TX_HASH.name: TX_HASH
TX_HASH.comment: Transaction hash is a unique 66-character identifier that is generated when a transaction is executed.

FROM_ADDRESS.name: FROM_ADDRESS
FROM_ADDRESS.comment: The sending address of this transaction.

TO_ADDRESS.name: TO_ADDRESS
TO_ADDRESS.comment: The receiving address of this transaction. This can be a contract address.

ORIGIN_FUNCTION_SIGNATURE.name: ORIGIN_FUNCTION_SIGNATURE
ORIGIN_FUNCTION_SIGNATURE.comment: The function signature of the call that triggered this transaction.

VALUE.name: VALUE
VALUE.comment: The value transacted in the native asset.

VALUE_PRECISE_RAW.name: VALUE_PRECISE_RAW
VALUE_PRECISE_RAW.comment: The precise, unadjusted amount of the transaction. This is returned as a string to avoid precision loss.

VALUE_PRECISE.name: VALUE_PRECISE
VALUE_PRECISE.comment: The precise, adjusted amount of the transaction. This is returned as a string to avoid precision loss.

TX_FEE.name: TX_FEE
TX_FEE.comment: Amount paid to validate the transaction in the native asset.

TX_FEE_PRECISE.name: TX_FEE_PRECISE
TX_FEE_PRECISE.comment: The precise amount of the transaction fee. This is returned as a string to avoid precision loss.

TX_SUCCEEDED.name: TX_SUCCEEDED
TX_SUCCEEDED.comment: Whether the transaction was successful, returned as a boolean.

NONCE.name: NONCE
NONCE.comment: The number of transactions sent from a given address.

TX_POSITION.name: TX_POSITION
TX_POSITION.comment: The position of the transaction within the block.

INPUT_DATA.name: INPUT_DATA
INPUT_DATA.comment: This column contains additional data for this transaction, and is commonly used as part of a contract interaction or as a message to the recipient.

GAS_PRICE.name: GAS_PRICE
GAS_PRICE.comment: Cost per unit of gas in Gwei.

GAS_LIMIT.name: GAS_LIMIT
GAS_LIMIT.comment: Maximum amount of gas allocated for the transaction.

CUMULATIVE_GAS_USED.name: CUMULATIVE_GAS_USED
CUMULATIVE_GAS_USED.comment: The total amount of gas used when this transaction was executed in the block.

EFFECTIVE_GAS_PRICE.name: EFFECTIVE_GAS_PRICE
EFFECTIVE_GAS_PRICE.comment: The total base charge plus tip paid for each unit of gas, in Gwei.

MAX_FEE_PER_GAS.name: MAX_FEE_PER_GAS
MAX_FEE_PER_GAS.comment: The maximum fee per gas of the transaction, in Gwei.

MAX_PRIORITY_FEE_PER_GAS.name: MAX_PRIORITY_FEE_PER_GAS
MAX_PRIORITY_FEE_PER_GAS.comment: The maximum priority fee per gas of the transaction, in Gwei.

L1_FEE.name: L1_FEE
L1_FEE.comment: The L1 portion of fees paid.

L1_FEE_PRECISE_RAW.name: L1_FEE_PRECISE_RAW
L1_FEE_PRECISE_RAW.comment: The raw l1 fee for the transaction, in Gwei.

L1_FEE_PRECISE.name: L1_FEE_PRECISE
L1_FEE_PRECISE.comment: The precise l1 fee for the transaction, in Gwei.

L1_FEE_SCALAR.name: L1_FEE_SCALAR
L1_FEE_SCALAR.comment: This value covers the change in L1 gas price between the time the transaction is submitted and when it is published from the OP stack L2 chain.

L1_GAS_USED.name: L1_GAS_USED
L1_GAS_USED.comment: The gas used on L1 to publish the transaction from the OP stack L2 chain.

L1_GAS_PRICE.name: L1_GAS_PRICE
L1_GAS_PRICE.comment: The gas price for L1 transactions when the transaction was processed from the OP stack L2 chain.

MINT.name: MINT
MINT.comment: The minting event associated with the transaction

ETH_VALUE.name: ETH_VALUE
ETH_VALUE.comment: The eth value for the transaction

R.name: R
R.comment: The r value of the transaction signature.

S.name: S
S.comment: The s value of the transaction signature.

V.name: V
V.comment: The v value of the transaction signature.

SOURCE_HASH.name: SOURCE_HASH
SOURCE_HASH.comment: The hash of the source transaction that created this transaction

FACT_TRANSACTIONS_ID.name: FACT_TRANSACTIONS_ID
FACT_TRANSACTIONS_ID.comment: The unique identifier for each row in the table.

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The UTC timestamp at which the row was inserted into the table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The UTC timestamp at which the row was last modified.

metadata.name: FACT_EVENT_LOGS
metadata.comment: This table contains flattened event logs from transactions on this EVM blockchain. Transactions may have multiple events, which are denoted by the event index for a transaction hash. Therefore, this table is unique on the combination of transaction hash and event index. Please see `fact_decoded_event_logs` or `ez_decoded_event_logs` for the decoded event logs.

BLOCK_NUMBER.name: BLOCK_NUMBER
BLOCK_NUMBER.comment: Also known as block height. The block number, which indicates the length of the blockchain, increases after the addition of each new block.

BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.name: BLOCK_TIMESTAMP
BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.comment: The date and time at which the block was produced.

TX_HASH.name: TX_HASH
TX_HASH.comment: Transaction hash is a unique 66-character identifier that is generated when a transaction is executed.

TX_POSITION.name: TX_POSITION
TX_POSITION.comment: The position of the transaction within the block.

CONTRACT_ADDRESS.name: CONTRACT_ADDRESS
CONTRACT_ADDRESS.comment: The address interacted with for a given event.

TOPIC_0.name: TOPIC_0
TOPIC_0.comment: The first topic of the event, which is a unique identifier for the event.

TOPIC_1.name: TOPIC_1
TOPIC_1.comment: The second topic of the event, if applicable.

TOPIC_2.name: TOPIC_2
TOPIC_2.comment: The third topic of the event, if applicable.

TOPIC_3.name: TOPIC_3
TOPIC_3.comment: The fourth topic of the event, if applicable.

EVENT_REMOVED.name: EVENT_REMOVED
EVENT_REMOVED.comment: Whether the event has been removed from the transaction.

ORIGIN_FROM_ADDRESS.name: ORIGIN_FROM_ADDRESS
ORIGIN_FROM_ADDRESS.comment: The sending address of this transaction.

ORIGIN_TO_ADDRESS.name: ORIGIN_TO_ADDRESS
ORIGIN_TO_ADDRESS.comment: The receiving address of this transaction. This can be a contract address.

ORIGIN_FUNCTION_SIGNATURE.name: ORIGIN_FUNCTION_SIGNATURE
ORIGIN_FUNCTION_SIGNATURE.comment: The function signature of the contract call that triggered this transaction.

TX_SUCCEEDED.name: TX_SUCCEEDED
TX_SUCCEEDED.comment: Whether the transaction was successful, returned as a boolean.

FACT_EVENT_LOGS_ID.name: FACT_EVENT_LOGS_ID
FACT_EVENT_LOGS_ID.comment: The unique identifier for each row in the table.

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The UTC timestamp at which the row was inserted into the table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The UTC timestamp at which the row was last modified.

metadata.name: EZ_EDGE_TRADES
metadata.comment: All edge trades paired with the associated trader/subaccount.

BLOCK_NUMBER.name: BLOCK_NUMBER
BLOCK_NUMBER.comment: Also known as block height. The block number, which indicates the length of the blockchain, increases after the addition of each new block.

BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.name: BLOCK_TIMESTAMP
BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.comment: The date and time at which the block was produced.

TX_HASH.name: TX_HASH
TX_HASH.comment: Transaction hash is a unique 66-character identifier that is generated when a transaction is executed. This field will not be unique in this table, as a given transaction can include multiple events.

EDGE_DIGEST.name: EDGE_DIGEST
EDGE_DIGEST.comment: The identifier for a specific trade, this can be split across two or more base deltas in order to fill the entire amount of the trade.

USER_DIGEST.name: USER_DIGEST
USER_DIGEST.comment: The identifier for a specific trade, this can be split across two or more base deltas in order to fill the entire amount of the trade.

TRADER.name: TRADER
TRADER.comment: The wallet address of the trader, there can be multiple subaccounts associated with a trader.

SUBACCOUNT.name: SUBACCOUNT
SUBACCOUNT.comment: Independent Vertex account of trader with its own margin, balance, positions, and trades. Any wallet can open an arbitrary number of these. Risk is not carried over from subaccount to subaccount.

SYMBOL.name: SYMBOL
SYMBOL.comment: The specific Vertex product symbol, if it is a futures product it will have a -PERP suffix.

EDGE_ORDER_TYPE.name: EDGE_ORDER_TYPE
EDGE_ORDER_TYPE.comment:
Decode from raw expiration number to binary then converted back to int from the most significant two bits: 
0 ⇒ Default order, where it will attempt to take from the book and then become a resting limit order if there is quantity remaining
1 ⇒ Immediate-or-cancel order, which is the same as a default order except it doesn’t become a resting limit order
2 ⇒ Fill-or-kill order, which is the same as an IOC order except either the entire order has to be filled or none of it.
3 ⇒ Post-only order, where the order is not allowed to take from the book. An error is returned if the order would cross the bid ask spread.

USER_ORDER_TYPE.name: USER_ORDER_TYPE
USER_ORDER_TYPE.comment:
Decode from raw expiration number to binary then converted back to int from the most significant two bits: 
0 ⇒ Default order, where it will attempt to take from the book and then become a resting limit order if there is quantity remaining
1 ⇒ Immediate-or-cancel order, which is the same as a default order except it doesn’t become a resting limit order
2 ⇒ Fill-or-kill order, which is the same as an IOC order except either the entire order has to be filled or none of it.
3 ⇒ Post-only order, where the order is not allowed to take from the book. An error is returned if the order would cross the bid ask spread.

EDGE_TRADE_TYPE.name: EDGE_TRADE_TYPE
EDGE_TRADE_TYPE.comment: They type of trade taken, long/short for perps or buy/sell for spot.

USER_TRADE_TYPE.name: USER_TRADE_TYPE
USER_TRADE_TYPE.comment: They type of trade taken, long/short for perps or buy/sell for spot.

EDGE_IS_TAKER.name: EDGE_IS_TAKER
EDGE_IS_TAKER.comment: Boolean representing if the trader was the taker or maker.

USER_IS_TAKER.name: USER_IS_TAKER
USER_IS_TAKER.comment: Boolean representing if the trader was the taker or maker.

EDGE_PRICE_AMOUNT_UNADJ.name: EDGE_PRICE_AMOUNT_UNADJ
EDGE_PRICE_AMOUNT_UNADJ.comment: The price amount that the trade was executed at.

USER_PRICE_AMOUNT_UNADJ.name: USER_PRICE_AMOUNT_UNADJ
USER_PRICE_AMOUNT_UNADJ.comment: The price amount that the trade was executed at.

EDGE_PRICE_AMOUNT.name: EDGE_PRICE_AMOUNT
EDGE_PRICE_AMOUNT.comment: The price amount that the trade was executed at, decimal adjusted. All amounts and prices are adjusted 18 decimals points regardless of underlying asset contract.

USER_PRICE_AMOUNT.name: USER_PRICE_AMOUNT
USER_PRICE_AMOUNT.comment: The price amount that the trade was executed at, decimal adjusted. All amounts and prices are adjusted 18 decimals points regardless of underlying asset contract.

EDGE_AMOUNT_UNADJ.name: EDGE_AMOUNT_UNADJ
EDGE_AMOUNT_UNADJ.comment: The total size of the trade in units of the asset being traded.

USER_AMOUNT_UNADJ.name: USER_AMOUNT_UNADJ
USER_AMOUNT_UNADJ.comment: The total size of the trade in units of the asset being traded.

EDGE_AMOUNT.name: EDGE_AMOUNT
EDGE_AMOUNT.comment: The total size of the trade in units of the asset being traded across one digest, decimal adjusted. All amounts and prices are adjusted 18 decimals points regardless of underlying asset contract.

USER_AMOUNT.name: USER_AMOUNT
USER_AMOUNT.comment: The total size of the trade in units of the asset being traded across one digest, decimal adjusted. All amounts and prices are adjusted 18 decimals points regardless of underlying asset contract.

EDGE_AMOUNT_USD.name: EDGE_AMOUNT_USD
EDGE_AMOUNT_USD.comment: The size of the trade in USD. Base Delta multiplied by the price amount.

USER_AMOUNT_USD.name: USER_AMOUNT_USD
USER_AMOUNT_USD.comment: The size of the trade in USD. Base Delta multiplied by the price amount.

EDGE_FEE_AMOUNT.name: EDGE_FEE_AMOUNT
EDGE_FEE_AMOUNT.comment: The fees on the trade, decimal adjusted. All amounts and prices are adjusted 18 decimals points regardless of underlying asset contract.

USER_FEE_AMOUNT.name: USER_FEE_AMOUNT
USER_FEE_AMOUNT.comment: The fees on the trade, decimal adjusted. All amounts and prices are adjusted 18 decimals points regardless of underlying asset contract.

EDGE_BASE_DELTA_AMOUNT_UNADJ.name: EDGE_BASE_DELTA_AMOUNT_UNADJ
EDGE_BASE_DELTA_AMOUNT_UNADJ.comment: Represents the net change in the total quantity of orders at a particular price level, the sum of these across the same digest is equal to the amount. This is the first currency listed in the pair and acts as the reference point for the exchange rate, in this case the crypto asset trading against USDC.

USER_BASE_DELTA_AMOUNT_UNADJ.name: USER_BASE_DELTA_AMOUNT_UNADJ
USER_BASE_DELTA_AMOUNT_UNADJ.comment: Represents the net change in the total quantity of orders at a particular price level, the sum of these across the same digest is equal to the amount. This is the first currency listed in the pair and acts as the reference point for the exchange rate, in this case the crypto asset trading against USDC.

EDGE_BASE_DELTA_AMOUNT.name: EDGE_BASE_DELTA_AMOUNT
EDGE_BASE_DELTA_AMOUNT.comment: Represents the net change in the total quantity of orders at a particular price level, decimal adjusted. All amounts and prices are adjusted 18 decimals points regardless of underlying asset contract. The sum of these across the same digest is equal to the amount. This is the first currency listed in the pair and acts as the reference point for the exchange rate, in this case the crypto asset trading against USDC.

USER_BASE_DELTA_AMOUNT.name: USER_BASE_DELTA_AMOUNT
USER_BASE_DELTA_AMOUNT.comment: Represents the net change in the total quantity of orders at a particular price level, decimal adjusted. All amounts and prices are adjusted 18 decimals points regardless of underlying asset contract. The sum of these across the same digest is equal to the amount. This is the first currency listed in the pair and acts as the reference point for the exchange rate, in this case the crypto asset trading against USDC.

EDGE_QUOTE_DELTA_AMOUNT_UNADJ.name: EDGE_QUOTE_DELTA_AMOUNT_UNADJ
EDGE_QUOTE_DELTA_AMOUNT_UNADJ.comment: A positive value is an increase in spread and a negative value is a decrease in spread. Quote is currency used to express the value of the base currency. It's often the more well-known or stable currency in the pair. In this case, USDC.

USER_QUOTE_DELTA_AMOUNT_UNADJ.name: USER_QUOTE_DELTA_AMOUNT_UNADJ
USER_QUOTE_DELTA_AMOUNT_UNADJ.comment: A positive value is an increase in spread and a negative value is a decrease in spread. Quote is currency used to express the value of the base currency. It's often the more well-known or stable currency in the pair. In this case, USDC.

EDGE_QUOTE_DELTA_AMOUNT.name: EDGE_QUOTE_DELTA_AMOUNT
EDGE_QUOTE_DELTA_AMOUNT.comment: The net change in the best bid and best ask prices in the order book, decimal adjusted. All amounts and prices are adjusted 18 decimals points regardless of underlying asset contract. A positive value is an increase in spread and a negative value is a decrease in spread. Quote is currency used to express the value of the base currency. It's often the more well-known or stable currency in the pair. In this case, USDC.

USER_QUOTE_DELTA_AMOUNT.name: USER_QUOTE_DELTA_AMOUNT
USER_QUOTE_DELTA_AMOUNT.comment: The net change in the best bid and best ask prices in the order book, decimal adjusted. All amounts and prices are adjusted 18 decimals points regardless of underlying asset contract. A positive value is an increase in spread and a negative value is a decrease in spread. Quote is currency used to express the value of the base currency. It's often the more well-known or stable currency in the pair. In this case, USDC.

EZ_EDGE_TRADES_ID.name: EZ_EDGE_TRADES_ID
EZ_EDGE_TRADES_ID.comment: The unique identifier for each row in the table.

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The UTC timestamp at which the row was inserted into the table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The UTC timestamp at which the row was last modified.

metadata.name: FACT_BLOCKS
metadata.comment: This table contains block level data for this EVM blockchain. This table can be used to analyze trends at a block level, for example gas fees vs. total transactions over time. For more information, please see [Etherscan Resources](https://etherscan.io/directory/Learning_Resources/Ethereum) or [The Ethereum Organization](https://ethereum.org/en/developers/docs/blocks/)

BLOCK_NUMBER.name: BLOCK_NUMBER
BLOCK_NUMBER.comment: Also known as block height. The block number, which indicates the length of the blockchain, increases after the addition of each new block.

BLOCK_HASH.name: BLOCK_HASH
BLOCK_HASH.comment: The hash of the block header for a given block.

BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.name: BLOCK_TIMESTAMP
BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.comment: The date and time at which the block was produced.

NETWORK.name: NETWORK
NETWORK.comment: The network on the blockchain used by a transaction.

TX_COUNT.name: TX_COUNT
TX_COUNT.comment: Total number of transactions within a block.

SIZE.name: SIZE
SIZE.comment: Block size, which is determined by a given block's gas limit.

MINER.name: MINER
MINER.comment: The address of the beneficiary to whom the mining rewards were given

MIX_HASH.name: MIX_HASH
MIX_HASH.comment: A string of a 256-bit hash encoded as a hexadecimal

EXTRA_DATA.name: EXTRA_DATA
EXTRA_DATA.comment: Any data included by the miner for a given block.

PARENT_HASH.name: PARENT_HASH
PARENT_HASH.comment: The hash of the block from which a given block is generated. Also known as the parent block.

GAS_USED.name: GAS_USED
GAS_USED.comment: Total gas used in the block.

GAS_LIMIT.name: GAS_LIMIT
GAS_LIMIT.comment: Total gas limit provided by all transactions in the block.

BASE_FEE_PER_GAS.name: BASE_FEE_PER_GAS
BASE_FEE_PER_GAS.comment: A string of the base fee encoded in hexadecimal format. Please note that this response field will not be included in a block requested before the EIP-1559 upgrade

DIFFICULTY.name: DIFFICULTY
DIFFICULTY.comment: The effort required to mine the block.

TOTAL_DIFFICULTY.name: TOTAL_DIFFICULTY
TOTAL_DIFFICULTY.comment: Total difficulty of the chain at a given block.

SHA3_UNCLES.name: SHA3_UNCLES
SHA3_UNCLES.comment: The mechanism which Ethereum Javascript RLP encodes an empty string.

UNCLE_BLOCKS.name: UNCLE_BLOCKS
UNCLE_BLOCKS.comment: Uncle blocks occur when two blocks are mined and broadcasted at the same time, with the same block number. The block validated across the most nodes will be added to the primary chain, and the other one becomes an uncle block. Miners do receive rewards for uncle blocks.

NONCE.name: NONCE
NONCE.comment: Block nonce is a value used during mining to demonstrate proof of work for a given block.

RECEIPTS_ROOT.name: RECEIPTS_ROOT
RECEIPTS_ROOT.comment: The root of the state trie.

STATE_ROOT.name: STATE_ROOT
STATE_ROOT.comment: The root of the final state trie of the block

TRANSACTIONS_ROOT.name: TRANSACTIONS_ROOT
TRANSACTIONS_ROOT.comment: The root of the transaction trie of the block

LOGS_BLOOM.name: LOGS_BLOOM
LOGS_BLOOM.comment: The bloom filter for the logs of the block.

FACT_BLOCKS_ID.name: FACT_BLOCKS_ID
FACT_BLOCKS_ID.comment: The unique identifier for each row in the table.

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The UTC timestamp at which the row was inserted into the table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The UTC timestamp at which the row was last modified.

metadata.name: EZ_CLEARING_HOUSE_EVENTS
metadata.comment: Vertex’s on-chain clearinghouse operates as the hub combining perpetual and spot markets, collateral, and risk calculations into a single integrated system. The events in this table track when a wallet either deposits or withdraws from the clearinghouse contract.

BLOCK_NUMBER.name: BLOCK_NUMBER
BLOCK_NUMBER.comment: Also known as block height. The block number, which indicates the length of the blockchain, increases after the addition of each new block.

BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.name: BLOCK_TIMESTAMP
BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.comment: The date and time at which the block was produced.

TX_HASH.name: TX_HASH
TX_HASH.comment: Transaction hash is a unique 66-character identifier that is generated when a transaction is executed. This field will not be unique in this table, as a given transaction can include multiple events.

CONTRACT_ADDRESS.name: CONTRACT_ADDRESS
CONTRACT_ADDRESS.comment: The address interacted with for a given event.

EVENT_NAME.name: EVENT_NAME
EVENT_NAME.comment: The name of the event, as defined in the contract ABI.

ORIGIN_FUNCTION_SIGNATURE.name: ORIGIN_FUNCTION_SIGNATURE
ORIGIN_FUNCTION_SIGNATURE.comment: The function signature of the contract call that triggered this transaction.

ORIGIN_FROM_ADDRESS.name: ORIGIN_FROM_ADDRESS
ORIGIN_FROM_ADDRESS.comment: The from address at the transaction level.

ORIGIN_TO_ADDRESS.name: ORIGIN_TO_ADDRESS
ORIGIN_TO_ADDRESS.comment: The to address at the transaction level.

SYMBOL.name: SYMBOL
SYMBOL.comment: The specific Vertex product symbol, if it is a futures product it will have a -PERP suffix.

TRADER.name: TRADER
TRADER.comment: The wallet address of the trader, there can be multiple subaccounts associated with a trader.

SUBACCOUNT.name: SUBACCOUNT
SUBACCOUNT.comment: Independent Vertex account of trader with its own margin, balance, positions, and trades. Any wallet can open an arbitrary number of these. Risk is not carried over from subaccount to subaccount.

TOKEN_ADDRESS.name: TOKEN_ADDRESS
TOKEN_ADDRESS.comment: The underlying asset token address deposited or withdrawn from the clearinghouse contract.

AMOUNT_UNADJ.name: AMOUNT_UNADJ
AMOUNT_UNADJ.comment: The total size of the trade in units of the asset being traded.

AMOUNT.name: AMOUNT
AMOUNT.comment: The total size of the trade in units of the asset being traded across one digest, decimal adjusted. All amounts and prices are adjusted 18 decimals points regardless of underlying asset contract.

AMOUNT_USD.name: AMOUNT_USD
AMOUNT_USD.comment: The size of deposit or withdraw in USD.

EZ_CLEARING_HOUSE_EVENTS_ID.name: EZ_CLEARING_HOUSE_EVENTS_ID
EZ_CLEARING_HOUSE_EVENTS_ID.comment: The unique identifier for each row in the table.

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The UTC timestamp at which the row was inserted into the table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The UTC timestamp at which the row was last modified.

metadata.name: DIM_CONTRACT_ABIS
metadata.comment:
This table contains the contract ABIs that we have sourced from Etherscan, the community, or bytecode matched. This table is the source of ABIs used in the `core__ez_decoded_event_logs` table.
We first try to source ABIs from Etherscan. If we cannot find an ABI on Etherscan, we will rely on user submissions. To add a contract to this table, please visit [here](https://science.flipsidecrypto.xyz/abi-requestor/).
If we are unable to locate an ABI for a contract from Etherscan or the community, we will try to find an ABI to use by matching the contract bytecode to a known contract bytecode we do have an ABI for.

CONTRACT_ADDRESS.name: CONTRACT_ADDRESS
CONTRACT_ADDRESS.comment: The unique address of the deployed contract.

ABI.name: ABI
ABI.comment: The JSON ABI for the contract.

ABI_SOURCE.name: ABI_SOURCE
ABI_SOURCE.comment: The source of the ABI. This can be the block explorer, `user_submitted`, or `bytecode_matched`.

BYTECODE.name: BYTECODE
BYTECODE.comment: The deployed bytecode of the contract.

metadata.name: EZ_NFT_TRANSFERS
metadata.comment: This table contains NFT transfer events on this EVM blockchain.

BLOCK_NUMBER.name: BLOCK_NUMBER
BLOCK_NUMBER.comment: Also known as block height. The block number, which indicates the length of the blockchain, increases after the addition of each new block.

BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.name: BLOCK_TIMESTAMP
BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.comment: The date and time at which the block was produced.

TX_HASH.name: TX_HASH
TX_HASH.comment: Transaction hash is a unique 66-character identifier that is generated when a transaction is executed.

TX_POSITION.name: TX_POSITION
TX_POSITION.comment: The position of the transaction within the block.

INTRA_EVENT_INDEX.name: INTRA_EVENT_INDEX
INTRA_EVENT_INDEX.comment: The order of events within a single event index. This is primarily used for ERC1155 NFT batch transfer events.

TOKEN_TRANSFER_TYPE.name: TOKEN_TRANSFER_TYPE
TOKEN_TRANSFER_TYPE.comment: The type of token and NFT transfer event, for example `erc721_Transfer`, `erc1155_TransferSingle`, or `erc1155_TransferBatch`.

IS_MINT.name: IS_MINT
IS_MINT.comment: Whether the event is a mint event.

FROM_ADDRESS.name: FROM_ADDRESS
FROM_ADDRESS.comment: The sending address of this transfer.

TO_ADDRESS.name: TO_ADDRESS
TO_ADDRESS.comment: The receiving address of this transfer. This can be a contract address.

CONTRACT_ADDRESS.name: CONTRACT_ADDRESS
CONTRACT_ADDRESS.comment: The address of the contract that the event log was emitted from.

TOKEN_ID.name: TOKEN_ID
TOKEN_ID.comment: The token ID for this NFT contract.

QUANTITY.name: QUANTITY
QUANTITY.comment: The number of NFTs transferred in this event by token_id. All ERC-721 tokens will have a value of 1 while ERC-1155 tokens may have a value of 1 or greater.

TOKEN_STANDARD.name: TOKEN_STANDARD
TOKEN_STANDARD.comment: The type of token transferred in this event, for example `erc721`, `erc1155`, or `erc20`.

NAME.name: NAME
NAME.comment: The name of the NFT project. This field, along with metadata, will be filled in over time.

ORIGIN_FUNCTION_SIGNATURE.name: ORIGIN_FUNCTION_SIGNATURE
ORIGIN_FUNCTION_SIGNATURE.comment: The function signature of the call that triggered this transaction.

ORIGIN_FROM_ADDRESS.name: ORIGIN_FROM_ADDRESS
ORIGIN_FROM_ADDRESS.comment: The from address at the transaction level.

ORIGIN_TO_ADDRESS.name: ORIGIN_TO_ADDRESS
ORIGIN_TO_ADDRESS.comment: The to address at the transaction level.

EZ_NFT_TRANSFERS_ID.name: EZ_NFT_TRANSFERS_ID
EZ_NFT_TRANSFERS_ID.comment: The unique identifier for each row in the table.

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The UTC timestamp at which the row was inserted into the table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The UTC timestamp at which the row was last modified.

metadata.name: EZ_ASSET_METADATA
metadata.comment: A convenience table holding prioritized asset metadata and other relevant details pertaining to each token_address and native asset. This data set is highly curated and contains metadata for one unique asset per blockchain.

TOKEN_ADDRESS.name: TOKEN_ADDRESS
TOKEN_ADDRESS.comment: The specific address representing the asset on this EVM blockchain. This will be NULL if referring to a native asset.

ASSET_ID.name: ASSET_ID
ASSET_ID.comment: The unique identifier representing the asset.

DECIMALS.name: DECIMALS
DECIMALS.comment: The number of decimals for the token contract on this EVM blockchain.

BLOCKCHAIN.name: BLOCKCHAIN
BLOCKCHAIN.comment: The Blockchain, Network, or Platform for this asset.

IS_NATIVE.name: IS_NATIVE
IS_NATIVE.comment: A flag indicating assets native to this EVM blockchain.

IS_DEPRECATED.name: IS_DEPRECATED
IS_DEPRECATED.comment: A flag indicating if the asset is deprecated or no longer supported by the provider.

EZ_ASSET_METADATA_ID.name: EZ_ASSET_METADATA_ID

metadata.name: EZ_DECODED_EVENT_LOGS
metadata.comment:
'For information on how to submit a contract for decoding, as well as how ABIs are sourced, please visit [here](https://science.flipsidecrypto.xyz/abi-requestor/). This model contains decoded event logs for contracts that we have an ABI for. Please note, this table does not include all event logs, only those that we have an ABI for. The `decoded_log` column is the easiest place to query decoded data. It is a JSON object, where the keys are the names of the event parameters, and the values are the values of the event parameters. You can select from this column using the following sample format, `decoded_log:from::string` or more generally, `decoded_log:<event_param>::datatype`. See below for a full sample query. The `full_decoded_logs` column contains the same information, as well as additional fields such as the datatype of the decoded data. You may need to laterally flatten this column to query the data.
      
Sample query for USDC Transfer events:
```sql select  tx_hash, block_number, contract_address, decoded_log:from::string as from_address, decoded_log:to::string as to_address, decoded_log:value::integer as value from ethereum.core.fact_decoded_event_logs where contract_address = lower('0xA0b86991c6218b36c1d19D4a2e9Eb0cE3606eB48') and block_number between 16400000 and 16405000 and event_name = 'Transfer' limit 50```'

BLOCK_NUMBER.name: BLOCK_NUMBER
BLOCK_NUMBER.comment: Also known as block height. The block number, which indicates the length of the blockchain, increases after the addition of each new block.

BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.name: BLOCK_TIMESTAMP
BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.comment: The date and time at which the block was produced.

TX_HASH.name: TX_HASH
TX_HASH.comment: Transaction hash is a unique 66-character identifier that is generated when a transaction is executed. This field will not be unique in this table, as a given transaction can include multiple events.

CONTRACT_ADDRESS.name: CONTRACT_ADDRESS
CONTRACT_ADDRESS.comment: The address interacted with for a given event.

CONTRACT_NAME.name: CONTRACT_NAME
CONTRACT_NAME.comment: The name of the contract, if the contract has a name() function.

EVENT_NAME.name: EVENT_NAME
EVENT_NAME.comment: The name of the event, as defined in the contract ABI.

DECODED_LOG.name: DECODED_LOG
DECODED_LOG.comment: The flattened decoded log, where the keys are the names of the event parameters, and the values are the values of the event parameters.

FULL_DECODED_LOG.name: FULL_DECODED_LOG
FULL_DECODED_LOG.comment: The full decoded log, including the event name, the event parameters, and the data type of the event parameters.

ORIGIN_FUNCTION_SIGNATURE.name: ORIGIN_FUNCTION_SIGNATURE
ORIGIN_FUNCTION_SIGNATURE.comment: The function signature of this transaction.

ORIGIN_FROM_ADDRESS.name: ORIGIN_FROM_ADDRESS
ORIGIN_FROM_ADDRESS.comment: The from address at the transaction level.

ORIGIN_TO_ADDRESS.name: ORIGIN_TO_ADDRESS
ORIGIN_TO_ADDRESS.comment: The to address at the transaction level.

EVENT_REMOVED.name: EVENT_REMOVED
EVENT_REMOVED.comment: Whether the event has been removed from the transaction.

EZ_DECODED_EVENT_LOGS_ID.name: EZ_DECODED_EVENT_LOGS_ID
EZ_DECODED_EVENT_LOGS_ID.comment: The unique identifier for each row in the table.

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The utc timestamp at which the row was inserted into the table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The utc timestamp at which the row was last modified.
